title: Treatment vs Baseline Confirmation
decision_question: Do the narrowed intervention signals beat baseline on fixed tasks strongly enough to drive promotion and demotion decisions?
hypothesis: Treatment evidence should become the decisive promotion bar only when paired baseline versus Sentrux-assisted runs show better task success, fewer escaped regressions, and higher top-action help on the same tasks and repos.

variants.name: Current batch arms
variants.description: Use the checked-in baseline and intervention arms already encoded in the live and replay batch manifests.

variants.name: Signal-specific confirmation
variants.description: Run fixed task sets where the promoted signal family is the main intervention difference rather than only repo-level treatment.
variants.policy_delta: Require signal-matched paired runs for promotion.

exit_bar: Use the same protocol for baseline and treatment across at least three repos and fixed task sets.
Publish signal-level effect-size reporting rather than only repo-level comparisons.
Do not promote a signal family until signal-matched treatment evidence is stronger than reviewed precision alone.
